question.text: If a credit card company charges 3% per month interest on revolving credit, the effective annual cost is approximately:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Effective annual rate = (1 + monthly rate)^12 - 1 = (1.03)^12 - 1 = 1.4258 - 1 = 42.58% ≈ 42.6%. Simple multiplication gives 36%, but compounding makes the true cost much higher. **The Trap:** 36% is the simple annual rate (3% × 12). The compounded rate is significantly higher due to interest-on-interest. **Cross-Exam Context:** Credit card interest calculation is a practical application of TVM tested in X-A (Ch 4). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Show this calculation to clients who carry credit card balances — 42.6% annual cost is higher than most equity returns. Pay off credit cards first!
